notes:
The game wont run out of the box and you will need to build or download a custom proton build for steam as well as update DXVK to V1.2.2 (latest build with specific fix for planet coaster)

Workarounds:-

1.Had to download and install DXVK v1.2.2 and update in proton 4.2 prefix
2.Downloaded and used a custom proton build which includes 3 wine patches for the game. (found at r/linux gaming)

Game runs ok at around 45fps average however there are some issues with the fonts (E.G Cannot read bank balance) so its best to play in sandbox mode for now.
title: Planet Coaster

notes: Game works pretty well performance wise with this custom proton build after added newest dxvk https://github.com/doitsujin/dxvk/issues/1091#issuecomment-502212138 but there is problem with some values having value "jd" and that makes game unplayable right now (can't see how much money etc).

This included profit, park budget, display settings, etc. Good framerate as well, it would be platinum if the number issue was working properly.

title: Planet Coaster

notes: Runs almost out of the box with Proton (see tweak below). Running the ultra graphics preset, I get mostly solid 60 FPS on career parks, which drop to around 20 FPS on the largest parks I could find on the workshop. I couldn't find any graphical or audio glitches after several hours of gameplay. Tweak needed to get numbers instead of "jd": Copy a Windows native ucrtbase.dll into the compatdata's Windows\system32 folder. Afterwards all values are displayed correctly and the game is 100% playable. It seems that wine's ucrtbase.dll doesn't like the "j" argument size specifier on the string formatting functions.
title: Planet Coaster

notes: The game will launch to a black screen and crash if particular USB devices are connected. In my case a ps4 controller would cause the game to crash. Also the file /windows/system32/ucrtbase.dll needed to be replaced with a windows version, as the one supplied with steam will cause numbers to show up as "%jd". Other than that the game runs fine.
title: Planet Coaster

notes: This is the first test I've done with this setup. Fresh Fedora Install, steam installed with latest version of Proton used (4.11.6). The loadings where definitly longer that the windows version but everything work out the box. Loaded my biggest parc (80% of a sandbox map full of coasters, peoples, props, mountains...). I did see a drop of performance of about 5-10% against the windows runs, but damn its impressive.
title: Planet Coaster

title: Planet Coaster

notes:
The game crashes on startup if I have my keyboard (Logitech G910) plugged in, but unplugging it, starting the game, then plugging it back in works. I also copied ucrtbase.dll from my Windows 10 install into the game's Wine prefix to fix the "jd" issue.

After that, the game runs great. Performance is about the same as it is in WIndows, and everything works as it should.
title: Planet Coaster

notes:
Works with a few minor tweaks

1. Applied "ucrtbase.dll" fix to get values in the game.

2. Had to unplug "Roccat Sova" Keyboard and Mouse immediately after launching game or the game will crash. I can replug and play once the game has loaded.

notes: the game starts now perfectly and to fix the jd values do this  https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/1538#issuecomment-529896867 and don't forget to put the dll from you home to the game folder... no need  to add it in the winecfg or so just put it there and it's fixed

notes: The game runs almost like in Windows (I don't know if I had few fps stuttering on a large map or if it was because of Proton), 100% playable with a good performance and graphics. The game actually works out of the box, but some numbers in game displays "jd" instead of the actual number.
